description: Per-screenshot allowlist for the US-158 image pipeline. Each entry pairs a stable logical 'id' (used as the canonical reference from <Screenshot id="…" /> in MDX and from src/sections/* in React) with an absolute-from-dev-repo-root 'source' path. The sync-content.mjs handleImageAllowlist transform reads this file, validates every source resolves under the sync-allowlist.json 'screenshots-final' boundary, runs Sharp to produce AVIF / WebP / PNG triplets plus a 400w WebP thumbnail per entry, and writes public/screenshots/manifest.json mapping id -> { avif, webp, png, thumb, width, height, alt, priority }. The per-asset 80 KB AVIF budget is enforced; a regression fails the build. Dev-repo filenames can rotate without breaking the site as long as this file updates alongside.

screenshots.alt: FramePath's hero view on Mac — screenplay on the left, scene navigator in the middle, shot list on the right.
Scene Editor on Mac — a Fountain-formatted scene open for editing in the Write stage.
Plan stage on Mac — three annotated shot cards side by side showing size, angle, and storyboard frame.
Storyboard board view on Mac — hand-drawn frames arranged in shot order across the scene.
Shoot Mode on iPad — current shot, scene context, and clapperboard side by side, ready for set.
